last_result.effect: EasyCrypt accepted a proof-state change; the refreshed view is based on the new committed state.
last_result.proof_state: The committed EasyCrypt proof state changed.

route_health.message: This looks like pRHL mid-surgery with a large postcondition. A common expert move is to use `conseq` to weaken to a smaller relational post, then use `sim` or automation.
evidence: `conseq` is useful after the frontier is already aligned enough to name a smaller relation
route_health.anti_route: Do not use `conseq` as a blind replacement for a missing call or loop invariant; if residuals need facts not carried by the last boundary, repair that boundary first.

structural_transitions.why_here: The current pRHL seq-cut exposes a large postcondition. Probe `wp.` if you want to test entry into the real post-wp suffix/surgery workbench.
structural_transitions.decision: Probe this tactic if you want to test entry into the post-wp surgery phase.
structural_transitions.after_commit: The next authoritative view will expose the real post-wp surgery workbench, so you do not need to mentally simulate now what will happen.
structural_transitions.if_wrong_after_commit: Use `undo_last_step` or `undo_to_checkpoint` if the committed route is wrong.

named_handles.why_visible: Use this as route-selection context; it is not a tactic to run as-is.
limitations: The tactic shape is hidden until the handle is callable at the current frontier.

frontier_blockers.left: 2 setup statement(s): c <- (c0, t); CTXT_Wrap.s <- CTXT_Wrap.s `|` fset1 c
frontier_blockers.right: 3 setup statement(s): m <- c; MACa.SUF_CMA.SUF_Wrap.s <- MACa.SUF_CMA.SUF_Wrap.s `|` fset1 (m, t0); t <- t0
frontier_blockers.effect: Residual statements remain after the live call frontier; their effects are not part of the callee obligation.

action.tactic_shape: seq 4 0 : <invariant preserving the call lemma postcondition>.
action.reason: the matching call is live but no paired edit-script step was available; expose this side's call frontier before calling the lemma

lifted_value_arguments.reason: lemma equality binds value slot `_k` to state expression `k{1}`
lemma equality binds value slot `_p` to state expression `p{1}`

precondition_atoms.reason: precondition is not a value-slot binding; it may need unfolding, arithmetic/list reasoning, or an existing hypothesis
value slot is explicitly tied to a program-state expression lifted by the generated exlim
value slot is explicitly tied to a program-state expression lifted by the generated exlim

expected_residual_atoms.reason: precondition is not a value-slot binding; it may need unfolding, arithmetic/list reasoning, or an existing hypothesis

lemma_precondition_coverage.strategy_boundary: Static coverage only: it tells the prover what facts the call will need, but it does not prove those facts or rank the proof strategy by lemma name.

rows.left: 2 setup statement(s): c <- (c0, t); CTXT_Wrap.s <- CTXT_Wrap.s `|` fset1 c
rows.right: 3 setup statement(s): m <- c; MACa.SUF_CMA.SUF_Wrap.s <- MACa.SUF_CMA.SUF_Wrap.s `|` fset1 (m, t0); t <- t0
rows.proof_read: These statements remain after the live call frontier. Their effects are checked after the call returns; a bare call obligation is checked before these residual statements execute.

residual_frontier.effect: These statements remain after the live call frontier and are checked after the call returns.
limitations: The surface reports suffix structure only; it does not choose whether to use a call, inline, wp, or another local tactic.

visible_hoare_handles.text: enc_corr: forall (_k : eK) (_p : ptxt), hoare[ E.enc : k = _k /\ p = _p ==> dec _k res = Some _p ]

packaging_evidence.effect: One-sided calls in a relational goal use single-procedure certificates; a Hoare certificate plus losslessness evidence can package the same local specification as a phoare certificate.
limitations: No losslessness or phoare certificate for the live procedure is visible in this view.

frontier_alignment.summary: Frontier alignment: setup statements appear before the current frontier; call sites are visible at the frontier; residual statements remain after a call frontier. Use this as a proof-reader map before choosing while/rnd/seq/call tactics.

first_instruction_alignment.right_first: no right-side setup before this frontier
first_instruction_alignment.proof_read: The visible frontier is one-sided; use a one-sided tactic, alignment step, or seq cut before applying a symmetric tactic.

rows.left: 3 setup statement(s): k <- CTXT_Wrap.k; p0 <- p; (ek, mk) <- k
rows.right: no right-side setup before this frontier
rows.proof_read: The left side has setup before the shared frontier; relate it to the precondition or carry the initialized state in the next invariant/cut.
Both sides expose the same call procedure; a named equiv call or an invariant call may apply at this frontier.
Both sides expose the same call procedure; a named equiv call or an invariant call may apply at this frontier.

rows.left: 2 setup statement(s): c <- (c0, t); CTXT_Wrap.s <- CTXT_Wrap.s `|` fset1 c
rows.right: 3 setup statement(s): m <- c; MACa.SUF_CMA.SUF_Wrap.s <- MACa.SUF_CMA.SUF_Wrap.s `|` fset1 (m, t0); t <- t0
rows.proof_read: These statements remain after the live call frontier. Their effects are checked after the call returns; a bare call obligation is checked before these residual statements execute.

procedure_navigation.how_to_use: Positions for choosing wp/sp depth, which `if` to rcond, and where the next loop/call frontier is. Facts only — pick the tactic and the branch direction yourself.

absorb_depth.sp_hint: sp 3 0 / wp -3 -0 absorbs the straight-line setup (3 left, 0 right) before the next frontier

checks: A runnable seq candidate is not evidence that the invariant is strong enough.

application_context.how_to_use: Use this section when constructing the concrete invariant inside `call (_: ...)`. This section is not a tactic. It is a checklist for constructing the concrete invariant before using `probe_tactic` or `commit_tactic`.

inspect_if_unsure.why: After you draft a concrete invariant, this preview shows the obligations it creates and can reveal missing facts or extra conjuncts that make the proof harder.

facts_and_diagnostics.fact_note: Only current-frontier facts are shown here; the full proof context artifact keeps the compiler/internal details.

notes.message: proof-state analysis sees live named call handle(s), but none is currently at EasyCrypt's last-call frontier (enc_eq). `call <lemma>` may fail until wp/seq/targeted transforms expose the matching call as the last statement.

proof-state analysis layer is call_site: 4 call site(s) and 1 callable lemma handle(s) are live (enc_eq). `inline *` is a broad global transformation here and can hide those handles; named calls and targeted inline are route context for comparison.
Some imported or cross-scope handles may need lookup if that route is being tested; use lookup_candidates for the concrete symbol instead of treating the whole list as a to-do queue.

evidence: `conseq` is useful after the frontier is already aligned enough to name a smaller relation
`conseq` is useful after the frontier is already aligned enough to name a smaller relation

limitations: does not prescribe a conseq, sim, wp, or skip script
reports residual program evidence before pure-tail obligations

inspect_lookup_handles.effect: All handles here are read-only manager requests; the committed EasyCrypt proof state stays unchanged.
inspect_lookup_handles.manager_note: Some requests may ask EasyCrypt or name resolution; wait for the manager result before choosing the next proof intent.

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need parsed goal shape, resolved names, or KB pattern hints.
The current cut or frontier context may expose a call route.
ask_manager_for.returns: call-ready/oracle-equiv context for the current frontier, plus the BODY of the procedure(s) called here (functor aliases resolved) so you can see which oracles/state your call invariant must preserve

ask_manager_for.use_when: You are considering `call (_: Inv)` and already have a concrete invariant expression you want to test first.
ask_manager_for.returns: a read-only preview of the obligations caused by that candidate invariant, including pre/init, call-preservation, oracle-side, postcondition, missing-fact signals, and extra conjuncts that may create avoidable obligations

ask_manager_for.use_when: The visible cut may depend on LHS/RHS statement alignment or missing live facts.
ask_manager_for.returns: swap/alignment context for the visible pRHL frontier

ask_manager_for.use_when: Need the valid form for call, seq, while, rnd, or rewrite.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid tactic forms and common traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: The visible cut/frontier may need indexed `sp i j` before branch tactics.
Mid-proof pRHL suffix surgery may need indexed `wp`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid indexed `wp` forms and suffix-alignment traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: Statement order may need a small `swap` before `sp`, `wp`, or `sim`.
A guarded branch may need `rcondt` after a case split or invariant fact.
A guarded branch may need `rcondf` after a case split or invariant fact.
A suffix proof may need `conseq` to weaken to a smaller relation before `sim`.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `conseq` forms and pRHL memory-annotation traps

ask_manager_for.use_when: One side may have an extra sample or need an explicit sample coupling.
A known statement-order mismatch may need an eager/lazy transformation.
ask_manager_for.returns: valid `eager` forms and when to prefer smaller surgery

lookup_candidates.use_when: Resolve exact signature for `enc_eq` only when this handle route is being tested.

items.label: Before call invariant #5
items.committed_tactic: call (_: ={glob E, glob M} /\ CTXT_Wrap.k{1} = (CMAa.ek, MACa.SUF_CMA.SUF_Wrap.k){2} /\ CTXT_Wrap.s{1} = MACa.SUF_CMA.SUF_Wrap.s{2} /\ (CTXT_Wrap.win{1} => MACa.SUF_CMA.SUF_Wrap.win{2})).
items.why_checkpoint: call invariant introduction point; later oracle/branch obligations often reveal missing preserved facts or an over-strong equality from this tactic
items.effect_if_selected: Undo committed tactic #5 and every committed tactic after it in this node.
